**(a)** If a Member (the \"**Selling Member**\") proposes to Transfer all or any portion of its membership interest to a third party (other than in a Permitted Transfer) and the Non-Offering Member has declined to exercise its right of first offer under Section 9.02, the other Member (the \"**Tag-Along Member**\") shall have the right, but not the obligation, to participate in such Transfer on the same terms and conditions as the Selling Member, by selling a proportionate share of the Tag-Along Member\'s membership interest to the proposed third-party purchaser.

**(b)** The Selling Member shall deliver written notice (the \"**Tag-Along Notice**\") to the Tag-Along Member not less than twenty (20) days prior to the anticipated closing date of the proposed Transfer, setting forth the material terms and conditions of the proposed Transfer. The Tag-Along Member shall have fifteen (15) days following receipt of the Tag-Along Notice to deliver written notice to the Selling Member of its election to exercise its tag-along rights. If the Tag-Along Member exercises its tag-along rights, the Selling Member shall not complete the Transfer unless the third-party purchaser agrees to purchase the Tag-Along Member\'s proportionate share of the offered interest on the same terms and conditions.

**(c)** The Tag-Along Member\'s proportionate share shall be determined by multiplying the total interest proposed to be Transferred by a fraction, the numerator of which is the Tag-Along Member\'s Percentage Interest and the denominator of which is the aggregate Percentage Interests of the Selling Member and the Tag-Along Member.

**Section 9.04 --- Drag-Along Rights**

**(a)** If Meridian (as the majority equity holder) proposes to Transfer its entire membership interest in the Company to a bona fide, arm\'s-length third-party purchaser (a \"**Drag-Along Sale**\"), Meridian shall have the right (the \"**Drag-Along Right**\") to require Kastor to Transfer its entire membership interest to such third-party purchaser on the same terms and conditions as Meridian\'s Transfer, including the same price per percentage point of Percentage Interest, the same form of consideration, and the same representations, warranties, indemnification obligations, and escrow arrangements.

**(b)** Meridian shall exercise its Drag-Along Right by delivering written notice (the \"**Drag-Along Notice**\") to Kastor not less than thirty (30) days prior to the anticipated closing date of the Drag-Along Sale, setting forth the material terms and conditions of the proposed Transfer and the identity of the proposed purchaser.

**(c)** Upon receipt of a valid Drag-Along Notice, Kastor shall be obligated to (i) sell its entire membership interest on the terms set forth in the Drag-Along Notice, (ii) execute and deliver all documents and instruments reasonably required to consummate the Drag-Along Sale, and (iii) take all other actions reasonably necessary to facilitate the Drag-Along Sale. Kastor shall not be required to make any representations or warranties in connection with the Drag-Along Sale other than representations and warranties regarding Kastor\'s title to its membership interest, due authorization, and absence of liens.

**Section 9.05 --- Involuntary Transfers**

**(a)** Upon the occurrence of any of the following events with respect to a Member (each, an \"**Involuntary Transfer Event**\"): (i) the bankruptcy, insolvency, receivership, or dissolution of such Member; (ii) the entry of a judgment, order, or decree of any court or governmental authority attaching, levying upon, or otherwise encumbering such Member\'s membership interest; (iii) the foreclosure upon or exercise of remedies with respect to a pledge, hypothecation, or other security interest in such Member\'s membership interest; or (iv) any other involuntary transfer or disposition of such Member\'s membership interest by operation of law, including by reason of divorce, death, or incapacity (in the case of a Member that is an individual) --- the non-affected Member (the \"**Non-Affected Member**\") shall have the option, exercisable within sixty (60) days following written notice of the Involuntary Transfer Event, to purchase the affected Member\'s entire membership interest at the Appraised Fair Market Value determined in accordance with Section 10.03.

**(b)** If the Non-Affected Member elects to exercise its purchase option under this Section 9.05, the closing of such purchase shall occur within ninety (90) days following the Non-Affected Member\'s exercise notice, and payment shall be made in immediately available funds at closing. The affected Member (or its trustee, receiver, executor, or other representative) shall cooperate in good faith to consummate such purchase.

**[ARTICLE X --- EXIT; BUY-SELL PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**Section 10.01 --- Trigger Events**

**\[PARTIALLY CONTESTED --- Meridian and Kastor have differing views on the scope of Trigger Events.\]**

A \"**Trigger Event**\" shall mean the occurrence of any of the following:

**(a)** a Change of Control of either Member;

**(b)** an unremedied material breach of this Agreement by either Member that continues beyond the applicable cure period (which cure period shall be thirty (30) days following written notice of such breach, or such longer period as may be reasonably necessary to cure such breach, *provided* that the breaching Member is diligently pursuing a cure);

**(c)** a Deadlock that remains unresolved after completion of the deadlock resolution procedures set forth in Section 5.01(d) (including executive escalation and mediation);

**(d)** an Involuntary Transfer Event as described in Section 9.05 with respect to either Member\'s membership interest.

*Meridian redline comment (Whitfield Ames & Trotter --- B. Falk): \"Meridian\'s position is that Trigger Events should be limited to Change of Control and material breach. Adding Deadlock as a Trigger Event incentivizes the minority holder to manufacture Deadlocks in order to trigger a forced buyout at favorable valuations. Involuntary Transfer Events are adequately addressed in Section 9.05 and need not be duplicated here.\"*

*Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano --- T. Escalante): \"Deadlock and involuntary Transfer should be included as Trigger Events for completeness. The buy-sell mechanism provides an orderly exit path for situations where the joint venture can no longer function effectively, and unresolved Deadlocks are a clear indicator of such dysfunction. Involuntary Transfers represent a fundamental change in the identity of a Member\'s owner and should trigger the same exit rights.\"*

**Section 10.02 --- Buy-Sell Initiation**

**(a)** Upon the occurrence of a Trigger Event, either Member (the \"**Initiating Member**\") may deliver written notice (the \"**Buy-Sell Notice**\") to the other Member (the \"**Responding Member**\") initiating the buy-sell procedures set forth in this Article X.

**(b)** The Buy-Sell Notice shall (i) identify the Trigger Event giving rise to the buy-sell initiation, (ii) state the Initiating Member\'s good-faith estimate of the Appraised Fair Market Value of the Company, and (iii) state whether the Initiating Member proposes to purchase the Responding Member\'s interest or to sell its own interest (or both, in the case of a \"shoot-out\" mechanism).

**(c)** The Responding Member shall have thirty (30) days following receipt of the Buy-Sell Notice (the \"**Response Period**\") to deliver a written response to the Initiating Member stating whether it elects to participate in the buy-sell procedures and, if so, its own good-faith estimate of the Appraised Fair Market Value of the Company.

**Section 10.03 --- Exit Valuation**

**\[CONTESTED PROVISION --- Competing tracked-changes markup from both sides\' counsel regarding valuation methodology.\]**

**(a) Valuation Methodology.**

The Appraised Fair Market Value of the Company shall be determined by a jointly-selected independent \"Big Four\" accounting firm (i.e., Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited, Ernst & Young Global Limited, KPMG International Limited, or PricewaterhouseCoopers International Limited, or their respective member firms) (the \"**Independent Appraiser**\"). If the Members are unable to agree on the Independent Appraiser within fifteen (15) days following the expiration of the Response Period, each Member shall select one (1) nationally recognized independent valuation firm within ten (10) business days, and those two firms shall jointly select a third firm to serve as the Independent Appraiser within a further ten (10) business days. The Independent Appraiser shall determine the fair market value of the Company using a discounted cash flow methodology, comparable transactions analysis, and such other valuation methodologies as the Independent Appraiser deems appropriate in its professional judgment, *provided* that the valuation shall not exceed a multiple of trailing twelve-month EBITDA as determined by reference to comparable transactions in the specialty chemicals sector. The Independent Appraiser shall render its determination within sixty (60) days of its appointment, and such determination shall be final and binding on the Members, absent manifest error.

*Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano --- T. Escalante): \"Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP is the Company\'s own auditor and has an ongoing engagement with the Company. It cannot serve as an independent appraiser for valuation purposes due to inherent conflicts of interest. Kastor requires a truly independent appraiser --- a Big Four firm with no existing relationship with the Company or either Member --- to ensure objectivity and credibility of the valuation.\"*

*Meridian redline comment (Whitfield Ames & Trotter --- B. Falk): \"A fixed 6.5× EBITDA multiple is consistent with Meridian\'s entry valuation, provides certainty and predictability, avoids the cost and delay of a full independent valuation, and eliminates the risk of a disputed appraisal. An open-ended independent valuation process introduces unnecessary delay (potentially three to four months), significant cost, and uncertainty. If the parties cannot agree on a methodology now, they will face even greater difficulty at the time of an exit.\"*

**(b)** For reference, comparable transactions data compiled by Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP (in its capacity as the Company\'s auditor, and not as an independent appraiser for purposes of this Section 10.03) indicates that EBITDA multiples for transactions in the specialty polymer coatings and specialty chemicals sector during the period from January 2023 through June 2025 have ranged from 6.2× to 8.1×, with a median multiple of 7.1×. The Members acknowledge that this data is provided for informational purposes only and shall not be binding on the Independent Appraiser or on the Members in connection with any valuation conducted under this Article X.

**(c) Costs.** All costs and expenses of the valuation process under this Section 10.03 (including the fees and expenses of the Independent Appraiser and, if applicable, of the valuation firms selected by the Members) shall be borne equally by the Members, or as otherwise allocated by the Management Committee or by the Independent Appraiser.

**Section 10.04 --- Buy-Sell Mechanics**

**(a) Offer and Response.** Upon determination of the Appraised Fair Market Value by the Independent Appraiser (or, if the fixed multiple methodology is ultimately adopted, by Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP), the Initiating Member shall deliver a written offer (the \"**Buy-Sell Offer**\") to the Responding Member to purchase the Responding Member\'s entire membership interest at a price equal to such Responding Member\'s pro-rata share of the Appraised Fair Market Value (determined by multiplying the Appraised Fair Market Value by the Responding Member\'s Percentage Interest). The Responding Member shall have sixty (60) days following receipt of the Buy-Sell Offer (the \"**Election Period**\") to elect, by written notice to the Initiating Member, either:

> \(i\) to accept the Buy-Sell Offer and sell its entire membership interest to the Initiating Member at the offered price; or
>
> \(ii\) to purchase the Initiating Member\'s entire membership interest at a price equal to the Initiating Member\'s pro-rata share of the Appraised Fair Market Value (the \"**Reverse Purchase**\").

If the Responding Member fails to deliver a timely election notice within the Election Period, the Responding Member shall be deemed to have elected to sell its membership interest to the Initiating Member at the offered price.

**(a-1) Baseball Arbitration Alternative.** As an alternative to the mechanism set forth in Section 10.04(a), and subject to the agreement of both Members at the time of the Buy-Sell Initiation, the Members may elect to submit the purchase price determination to \"baseball arbitration\" as follows: each Member shall submit to the Independent Appraiser a sealed, written statement setting forth such Member\'s proposed valuation of the Company (the \"**Sealed Valuation**\"). The Independent Appraiser shall, within thirty (30) days of receipt of both Sealed Valuations, select the Sealed Valuation that is closer to the Independent Appraiser\'s own independent determination of the Appraised Fair Market Value, and such selected Sealed Valuation shall be the Appraised Fair Market Value for all purposes of this Article X. The Independent Appraiser\'s selection shall be final and binding on both Members, absent manifest error.

*Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano --- T. Escalante): \"Baseball arbitration incentivizes both Members to submit reasonable valuations and avoids the risk of extreme or strategic pricing. Kastor proposes this as an alternative mechanism available by mutual consent.\"*

**(b) Closing.** The closing of the purchase and sale of membership interests pursuant to this Section 10.04 shall occur within ninety (90) days following the Responding Member\'s acceptance of the Buy-Sell Offer (or, in the case of a Reverse Purchase, within ninety (90) days following the Responding Member\'s election to purchase). At the closing, (i) the purchasing Member shall pay the purchase price to the selling Member in immediately available funds by wire transfer to an account designated by the selling Member, and (ii) the selling Member shall execute and deliver to the purchasing Member such instruments of assignment, transfer, and conveyance as are reasonably necessary to effect the transfer of the selling Member\'s entire membership interest. The selling Member shall represent and warrant at closing that (A) it is the sole beneficial owner of its membership interest, free and clear of all liens, claims, encumbrances, and restrictions, (B) the execution and delivery of the transfer documents have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate, limited liability company, or other organizational action, and (C) no consent, approval, or other action by any governmental authority or other Person is required in connection with such Transfer, other than as has been obtained.

**(c) Contributed IP Reversion.** Upon completion of a buy-sell transaction under this Article X, each Member\'s Contributed IP shall revert to the contributing Member in accordance with the following provisions:

> \(i\) The Meridian Contributed IP (as listed on Part A of Schedule 12.01) shall revert to Meridian, and the Company (or the purchasing Member, as applicable) shall execute and deliver to Meridian such instruments of assignment, transfer, and conveyance as are reasonably necessary to effect such reversion.
>
> \(ii\) The Kastor Contributed IP (as listed on Part B of Schedule 12.01) shall revert to Kastor, and the Company (or the purchasing Member, as applicable) shall execute and deliver to Kastor such instruments of assignment, transfer, and conveyance as are reasonably necessary to effect such reversion.
>
> \(iii\) The reversion of Contributed IP shall be subject to the license-back provisions of Section 12.05, which shall survive the dissolution of the Company and the completion of any buy-sell transaction under this Article X.

Both Members have agreed on the principle of Contributed IP reversion as set forth in this Section 10.04(c).

**Section 10.05 --- Liquidated Damages and Specific Performance**

**(a)** The Members acknowledge and agree that the membership interests in the Company are unique, that a breach of the buy-sell provisions of this Article X would cause irreparable harm to the non-breaching Member, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, each Member agrees that the other Member shall be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive relief to enforce the provisions of this Article X, without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bond or other security.

**(b)** If a Member fails to consummate a purchase or sale of membership interests as required by this Article X within the applicable time periods, the non-breaching Member shall be entitled to liquidated damages in an amount equal to five percent (5%) of the applicable purchase price, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.

**(c)** Any action for specific performance or injunctive relief under this Section 10.05 may be brought in the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware (or, if the Court of Chancery declines jurisdiction, in the Superior Court of the State of Delaware), and each Member hereby consents to the exclusive jurisdiction of such courts for purposes of any such action.

**[ARTICLE XI --- NON-COMPETITION AND NON-SOLICITATION]{.underline}**

**Section 11.01 --- Non-Competition**

**\[CONTESTED PROVISION --- Competing tracked-changes markup from both sides\' counsel regarding Restricted Period, Restricted Territory, and scope of restriction.\]**

**(a) Restricted Period.**

During the term of this Agreement and for a period of one (1) year following the date on which a Member ceases to hold any interest in the Company (the \"**Restricted Period**\"), each Member agrees as follows:

*Meridian redline comment (Whitfield Ames & Trotter --- B. Falk): \"A two-year non-compete is necessary to protect the JV\'s proprietary technology, customer relationships, and goodwill. Delaware and Texas courts routinely enforce two-year post-termination non-competition covenants in commercial joint venture and LLC member contexts. See, e.g., Boulanger v. Dunkin\' Donuts Inc., 815 F.2d 572 (1st Cir. 1987); Marsh USA Inc. v. Cook, 354 S.W.3d 764 (Tex. 2011) (enforcing two-year covenant in employment context). The two-year period is particularly warranted here given the substantial IP contributions and the risk that a departing Member could use JV-developed knowledge to compete immediately.\"*

*Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano --- T. Escalante): \"One year is adequate for commercial non-competes in the joint venture context. A two-year restriction is overreaching, particularly for a Swiss entity with global operations that extend well beyond the scope of the JV. Swiss competition law (Federal Act on Cartels and Other Restraints of Competition, KG) and EU competition law impose strict proportionality requirements on non-compete obligations, and a two-year restriction would likely be considered excessive under European standards. One year provides adequate protection while respecting Kastor\'s legitimate business interests.\"*

**(b) Restricted Territory.**

The \"**Restricted Territory**\" shall mean only those territories in which the Company had active sales (i.e., recognized revenue from product sales to end customers) during the twelve (12) months preceding the date on which the relevant Member ceases to hold any interest in the Company.

*Meridian redline comment (Whitfield Ames & Trotter --- B. Falk): \"North America is the JV\'s primary market and the territory where Meridian\'s proprietary coating formulations have the greatest commercial value. Limiting the Restricted Territory to territories where the Company had active sales creates a moving target and would exclude territories where the Company is actively developing market entry strategies (e.g., potential expansion into Mexico during the first two years of operations). A fixed North American territory provides certainty and adequate protection.\"*

*Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano --- T. Escalante): \"Kastor has global operations in specialty chemicals and cannot accept a blanket North American non-compete that would restrict Kastor\'s pre-existing business activities in markets where the JV has no presence. The Restricted Territory should be limited to territories where the JV has actually generated revenue, ensuring proportionality.\"*

**(c) Scope of Restriction.**

No Member shall, directly or through any Affiliate, engage in or assist any Person in engaging in the development, manufacture, marketing, distribution, or sale of the specific JV product lines (as defined and listed in Schedule 2.04) within the Restricted Territory during the Restricted Period.

*Meridian redline comment (Whitfield Ames & Trotter --- B. Falk): \"A broad scope covering \'all specialty polymer coatings\' is necessary to prevent indirect competition through adjacent product lines. A narrow restriction limited to \'specific JV product lines\' would allow a departing Member to develop a competing product that is functionally equivalent to a JV product but falls outside the defined product list.\"*

*Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano --- T. Escalante): \"A restriction covering \'all specialty polymer coatings\' is vastly overbroad and would effectively prohibit Kastor from conducting a significant portion of its existing global chemical business. The restriction must be limited to the specific product lines developed and commercialized by the JV, as defined in Schedule 2.04. This is the standard approach in joint venture non-competes.\"*

**(d) Exceptions.** Notwithstanding the foregoing, the restrictions set forth in this Section 11.01 shall not prohibit or restrict:

> \(i\) the acquisition and holding, as a passive investment, of less than five percent (5%) of the outstanding publicly traded equity securities of any Person that is engaged in activities that would otherwise be restricted by this Section 11.01;
>
> \(ii\) the continuation of any pre-existing product line of either Member that is not within the Business Purpose as defined in Schedule 2.04, *provided* that such Member does not expand such pre-existing product line into new product categories that fall within the Business Purpose during the Restricted Period;
>
> \(iii\) bona fide academic research, scientific publications, and presentations at industry conferences, *provided* that such activities do not involve the disclosure of Confidential Information of the Company; or
>
> \(iv\) any activity expressly approved in writing by the Management Committee.

**(e) Enforceability.** The Members acknowledge and agree that the non-competition covenants set forth in this Section 11.01 are reasonable and necessary to protect the legitimate business interests of the Company and the Members, including the Company\'s Intellectual Property, trade secrets, customer relationships, and goodwill. If any provision of this Section 11.01 is determined by a court of competent jurisdiction to be unenforceable by reason of its extending for too great a period of time, over too great a geographical area, or over too great a range of activities, such provision shall be interpreted to extend only over the maximum period of time, geographical area, or range of activities as to which it may be enforceable. The Members agree that any such court shall have the power to reform (i.e., \"blue-pencil\") the restrictive covenants set forth in this Section 11.01 to make them enforceable to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law. The Members acknowledge that the Company\'s principal place of business is in Texas, that this Agreement is governed by Delaware law, and that the enforceability of non-competition covenants may vary by jurisdiction. The Members further acknowledge that California Business and Professions Code § 16600 generally prohibits non-competition agreements but that exceptions may apply to members of limited liability companies and holders of partnership interests. The Members agree that the enforceability of this Section 11.01 shall be determined under Delaware law, consistent with Section 20.01 of this Agreement.

**Section 11.02 --- Non-Solicitation**

**(a)** During the term of this Agreement and during the Restricted Period, no Member shall, directly or indirectly through any Affiliate, agent, or representative: (i) solicit, recruit, or hire, or attempt to solicit, recruit, or hire, any employee, officer, or consultant of the Company; (ii) induce or attempt to induce any employee, officer, or consultant of the Company to terminate his or her employment or engagement with the Company; or (iii) interfere with the relationship between the Company and any of its employees, officers, or consultants.

**(b)** Notwithstanding the foregoing, the restrictions set forth in this Section 11.02 shall not apply to: (i) general advertisements or solicitations not specifically directed at employees, officers, or consultants of the Company (including postings on publicly available job boards, social media platforms, or a Member\'s corporate website); (ii) the hiring of any person who responds to such general advertisement or solicitation without having been specifically targeted or individually solicited; or (iii) the hiring of any person whose employment or engagement with the Company has been terminated (by either the Company or such person) for at least six (6) months prior to the date of such hiring.

**Section 11.03 --- Remedies for Breach**

**(a)** The Members acknowledge and agree that any breach of the covenants set forth in Sections 11.01 and 11.02 would cause irreparable harm to the Company and the non-breaching Member, that monetary damages alone would not be an adequate remedy for such breach, and that the non-breaching Member and the Company shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief (including temporary restraining orders, preliminary injunctions, and permanent injunctions) to enforce the provisions of Sections 11.01 and 11.02, without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bond or other security.

**(b)** The right to seek injunctive relief shall be in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other rights or remedies available to the non-breaching Member at law or in equity, including the right to recover damages.

**(c)** If any Member is found by a court of competent jurisdiction to have breached any of the covenants set forth in Sections 11.01 or 11.02, the Restricted Period applicable to such Member shall be extended by a period equal to the duration of such breach, so that the Company and the non-breaching Member receive the full benefit of the agreed-upon restrictive period.

**[ARTICLE XII --- INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY]{.underline}**

**Section 12.01 --- Contributed IP**

**(a) Meridian Contributed IP.** Meridian shall contribute, assign, and transfer to the Company the Meridian Contributed IP, consisting of twenty-three (23) items of Intellectual Property as listed on Part A of Schedule 12.01, including proprietary coating formulations embedded in JV products, patents, patent applications, trade secrets, and know-how. The agreed fair market value of the Meridian Contributed IP is Twenty-Eight Million Six Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$28,600,000), as determined by independent appraisal conducted by Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP, a copy of which appraisal report has been provided to Kastor. The Meridian Contributed IP constitutes the core coating technology upon which the Company\'s initial product lines will be based.

**(b) Kastor Contributed IP.** Kastor shall contribute, assign, and transfer to the Company the Kastor Contributed IP, consisting of nine (9) items of Intellectual Property as listed on Part B of Schedule 12.01, including manufacturing process documentation, process chemistry know-how, and related trade secrets used in JV operations. The Kastor Contributed IP includes proprietary manufacturing methodologies and process optimization techniques that will enable the Company to achieve industrial-scale production of the JV\'s product lines.

**(c) Ownership Representations.** Each contributing Member represents and warrants to the other Member and to the Company that (i) it is the sole legal and beneficial owner of its Contributed IP, free and clear of all liens, claims, encumbrances, security interests, licenses (other than non-exclusive licenses that do not conflict with the Company\'s use), and restrictions, (ii) it has full right, title, power, and authority to contribute, assign, and transfer such Contributed IP to the Company, (iii) such contribution, assignment, and transfer does not and will not infringe, misappropriate, or otherwise violate any Intellectual Property rights of any third party, and (iv) there are no pending or, to such Member\'s knowledge, threatened actions, claims, or proceedings challenging the ownership, validity, or enforceability of any item of Contributed IP.

**(d) Instruments of Transfer.** Each contributing Member shall execute and deliver to the Company, at or prior to the Closing Date, such instruments of assignment, transfer, and conveyance (including patent assignments, trademark assignments, and copyright assignments, in each case in recordable form) as are reasonably necessary to effectuate the transfer of the Contributed IP to the Company.

**Section 12.02 --- Co-Developed IP; Improvements**

**(a) Ownership of Co-Developed IP.** All Co-Developed IP created during the term of this Agreement shall be owned solely and exclusively by the Company. Inventorship of Co-Developed IP shall be determined in accordance with applicable patent law (including 35 U.S.C. § 116 for United States patent applications). Each Member shall cause its employees, officers, consultants, and seconded personnel who participate in the development of Co-Developed IP to execute written invention assignment agreements in favor of the Company, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Management Committee, obligating such individuals to assign all right, title, and interest in and to Co-Developed IP to the Company.

**(b) Improvements.** Improvements to Meridian Contributed IP and Kastor Contributed IP, respectively, that are developed by, funded by, or at the direction of the Company shall be owned solely by the Company. Each Member acknowledges that the Company may invest significant resources in improving the Contributed IP, and that such Improvements constitute valuable assets of the Company. Upon dissolution of the Company or the exit of a Member pursuant to Article X, the treatment of Improvements shall be governed by the license-back provisions of Section 12.05 and the allocation procedures set forth in Schedule 12.02. The Members acknowledge the existence of an \"improvements gap\" --- *i.e.*, the need for express agreement on the allocation of ownership and licensing rights in Improvements to Contributed IP upon the dissolution of the Company or the exit of a Member --- and agree that Schedule 12.02 shall set forth detailed provisions addressing this issue.

**(c) Identification and Documentation Procedures.** Schedule 12.02 shall set forth the procedures and criteria for identifying, documenting, and categorizing Co-Developed IP and Improvements, including (i) definitions of qualifying activities and funding thresholds for the creation of Co-Developed IP, (ii) inventorship determination procedures, (iii) documentation and reporting requirements (including quarterly updates to a Company IP register maintained by the CTO), and (iv) allocation and ownership rules applicable upon dissolution of the Company or the exit of a Member.

**Section 12.03 --- IP Prosecution and Enforcement**

**(a)** The Company shall be responsible for the preparation, prosecution, maintenance, and renewal of all patents, patent applications, trademark registrations, copyright registrations, and other IP registrations owned by the Company, including all Co-Developed IP. The Company shall engage qualified patent counsel and trademark counsel to handle the prosecution and maintenance of the Company\'s IP portfolio, subject to the oversight of the CTO and the Management Committee.

**(b)** All costs and expenses incurred in connection with the prosecution, maintenance, and renewal of the Company\'s IP portfolio shall be borne by the Company as a cost of operations.

**(c)** The Company shall be responsible for the enforcement of its IP rights, including the initiation and prosecution of any legal action to prevent or remedy infringement, misappropriation, or other unauthorized use of IP owned by the Company. If the Management Committee determines not to initiate enforcement action with respect to any item of Company IP, the contributing Member of any Contributed IP that is the subject of such infringement shall have the right (but not the obligation) to initiate enforcement action in its own name or in the name of the Company, at such Member\'s sole cost and expense, subject to the prior written notice to and reasonable cooperation of the other Member and the Company.

**(d)** Each Member shall cooperate fully with the Company in connection with the prosecution, maintenance, enforcement, and defense of the Company\'s IP rights, including by providing access to relevant documents, records, and personnel, and by executing and delivering such instruments and documents as may be reasonably necessary or appropriate.

**Section 12.04 --- IP Representations and Warranties**

Each Member represents and warrants to the other Member and to the Company, as of the Effective Date, that:

**(a)** to such Member\'s knowledge, the Contributed IP contributed by such Member does not infringe, misappropriate, or otherwise violate any Intellectual Property rights of any third party;

**(b)** all patents and patent applications included in such Member\'s Contributed IP are valid and enforceable (to the extent issued), and no actions, claims, or proceedings are pending or, to such Member\'s knowledge, threatened challenging the validity, enforceability, or scope of any such patents or patent applications;

**(c)** the Company will have freedom to operate with respect to the Contributed IP within the scope of the Business Purpose, and no license, consent, or authorization from any third party is required in connection with the Company\'s use of such Contributed IP in furtherance of the Business Purpose;

**(d)** there are no pending or, to such Member\'s knowledge, threatened actions, claims, demands, or proceedings by or against such Member relating to any item of Contributed IP; and

**(e)** such Member has not granted any exclusive license, option, right of first refusal, or other right to any third party with respect to any item of Contributed IP that would conflict with or restrict the Company\'s use of such Contributed IP.

**Section 12.05 --- IP License-Back on Dissolution or Exit**

**\[CONTESTED PROVISION --- Competing tracked-changes markup from both sides\' counsel. This is a core commercial issue. Meridian priority: prevent Kastor sublicensing to North American competitors. Kastor priority: broad license-back of Co-Developed IP with sublicensing rights in Kastor\'s territory.\]**

**(a) Reversion of Contributed IP.** Upon dissolution of the Company or the completion of a buy-sell transaction under Article X, each Member\'s Contributed IP shall revert to the contributing Member. The Company (or the purchasing Member, as applicable) shall execute and deliver to each contributing Member such instruments of assignment, transfer, and conveyance as are reasonably necessary to effect such reversion. This provision has been agreed by both Members.

**(b) License to Co-Developed IP and Improvements.**

Upon reversion of Contributed IP pursuant to Section 12.05(a), each Member shall receive a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable license to use the Co-Developed IP and any Improvements in all fields and territories worldwide, including the right to sublicense to third parties in such Member\'s respective territory (North America for Meridian; rest of world for Kastor).

*Meridian redline comment (Whitfield Ames & Trotter --- B. Falk): \"Kastor sublicensing rights in any territory --- but particularly in territories outside North America --- would enable Kastor to sublicense Co-Developed IP to Meridian\'s North American competitors, who could then use such IP to compete directly with Meridian in the North American market. This is a non-starter. This is Elaine Cordero\'s highest priority in this negotiation: Meridian will not accept any structure that permits Kastor to sublicense Co-Developed IP incorporating or derived from Meridian Contributed IP to any Person who competes with Meridian in North America. Meridian\'s position is that Meridian should receive an exclusive license (without sublicensing rights) to all Co-Developed IP and Improvements, and Kastor should receive only a narrow, non-exclusive, non-sublicensable license limited to Kastor\'s direct use outside North America.\"*

*Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano --- T. Escalante): \"A broad, symmetric license-back of Co-Developed IP to both parties is Kastor\'s top priority. Kastor has contributed critical manufacturing process documentation (nine (9) items of Contributed IP) that forms the foundation of the Company\'s manufacturing capabilities, and the Company will invest significant resources in developing Improvements to such process technology. Kastor should receive a full license-back of Co-Developed IP, including sublicensing rights in its territory (rest of world). Without sublicensing rights, the value of the license is substantially diminished, as Kastor\'s business model in many international markets relies on licensing and partnership arrangements with local manufacturers. Kastor is willing to accept territorial restrictions (Kastor: rest of world; Meridian: North America) but requires sublicensing rights in its territory.\"*

**(c) Scope of Sublicensing.**

Each Member shall have the right to sublicense Co-Developed IP and Improvements to third parties in its respective territory (Meridian: North America; Kastor: rest of world), *provided* that no Member shall sublicense Co-Developed IP or Improvements to a Person listed on Schedule 12.05 (Restricted Competitors) without the prior written consent of the other Member, which consent may be withheld in such other Member\'s sole and absolute discretion.

*Meridian redline comment (Whitfield Ames & Trotter --- B. Falk): \"Even with a Restricted Competitors carve-out, Kastor\'s proposed sublicensing rights would allow Kastor to sublicense to entities that are not currently listed on Schedule 12.05 but that may subsequently become competitors of Meridian. The Restricted Competitors list is inherently backward-looking and cannot anticipate future competitive threats. No sublicensing rights without prior written consent.\"*

*Kastor redline comment (Rennert Krieger Solano --- T. Escalante): \"The Restricted Competitors list can be updated from time to time by the Management Committee to address new competitive threats. The sublicensing restriction to Restricted Competitors provides adequate protection for Meridian while preserving Kastor\'s ability to monetize its interest in Co-Developed IP outside North America.\"*

**(d) Improvements to Contributed IP.** The Members acknowledge the need for express treatment of Improvements made to Meridian Contributed IP (contributed at an agreed fair market value of \$28,600,000 and likely subject to significant JV-funded improvements during the term of the Agreement) and Improvements made to Kastor Contributed IP during the JV term. The following principles shall apply:

> \(i\) Improvements to Contributed IP that are developed by, funded by, or at the direction of the Company shall be owned by the Company during the term of the Agreement.
>
> \(ii\) Upon dissolution of the Company or the exit of a Member pursuant to Article X, Improvements to a Member\'s Contributed IP shall be treated as Co-Developed IP for purposes of the license-back provisions of this Section 12.05, and the contributing Member and the non-contributing Member shall each receive a license to such Improvements on the terms set forth in Section 12.05(b) (as finally agreed).
>
> \(iii\) The allocation of ownership and licensing rights in Improvements upon dissolution or exit shall be further detailed in Schedule 12.02, which shall address the specific treatment of Improvements to each item of Contributed IP, including any necessary apportionment of value and royalty obligations.

Both Members have flagged the need for further negotiation and detail on this point, and the final terms of Section 12.05(d) and the corresponding provisions of Schedule 12.02 remain subject to agreement.

**(e) Survival.** The license-back provisions of this Section 12.05 (including the licenses granted, the sublicensing restrictions, and the treatment of Improvements) shall survive the dissolution of the Company, the termination of this Agreement, and the completion of any buy-sell transaction under Article X, and shall remain in full force and effect in perpetuity.

**[ARTICLE XIII --- RELATED-PARTY TRANSACTIONS; SUPPLY AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**Section 13.01 --- Kastor Supply Agreement**

**(a)** Kastor shall, on or before the Closing Date, enter into a supply agreement with the Company (the \"**Supply Agreement**\") for the supply of specified raw materials, chemical feedstocks, and intermediates to the Company on terms no less favorable to the Company than those available from unrelated third parties in bona fide, arm\'s-length transactions for comparable quantities and specifications.

**(b)** The initial term of the Supply Agreement shall be five (5) years from the Closing Date, with automatic renewal for successive one (1)-year periods unless terminated by either party upon at least twelve (12) months\' prior written notice. The Supply Agreement shall include customary provisions regarding pricing (including annual price adjustment mechanisms), quality specifications, delivery schedules, force majeure, remedies for non-conforming deliveries, and termination.

**(c)** The agreed value of the initial term of the Supply Agreement is Two Million Dollars (\$2,000,000), which shall be credited to Kastor\'s Capital Account as part of Kastor\'s Initial Capital Contribution pursuant to Section 4.01(b)(ii).

**(d)** The terms and conditions of the Supply Agreement (and any renewal, amendment, or modification thereof) shall be subject to review and approval by the Management Committee as a Related-Party Transaction in accordance with Section 13.02.

**Section 13.02 --- Arm\'s-Length Standard**

**(a)** All transactions between the Company, on the one hand, and either Member or any Affiliate of a Member, on the other hand (each, a \"**Related-Party Transaction**\"), shall be conducted on arm\'s-length terms that are no less favorable to the Company than those that would be obtained in a comparable transaction with an unrelated third party. The foregoing requirement applies to the Supply Agreement, any management services agreement, any technical services agreement, any administrative support arrangement, and any other contract, arrangement, or transaction between the Company and a Member or its Affiliate.

**(b)** All Related-Party Transactions shall be disclosed to the Management Committee in advance and shall be subject to the approval of the Management Committee, with the Managers appointed by the interested Member abstaining from the vote. The Management Committee may engage independent advisors to evaluate the terms of any proposed Related-Party Transaction.

**Section 13.03 --- Meridian Services Agreement**

If Meridian or any Affiliate of Meridian provides management services, technical services, administrative support, or other services to the Company, the terms and conditions of any such arrangement shall be set forth in a written services agreement approved by the Management Committee in accordance with Section 13.02. Such services agreement shall specify the scope of services, performance standards, fees and expenses (which shall be on arm\'s-length terms), term, termination provisions, and such other terms as the Management Committee deems appropriate. Any fees or expenses payable by the Company to Meridian or its Affiliates under such services agreement shall be disclosed as a Related-Party Transaction.

**[ARTICLE XIV --- DISSOLUTION AND WINDING UP]{.underline}**

**Section 14.01 --- Dissolution Events**

The Company shall be dissolved upon the earliest to occur of any of the following events (each, a \"**Dissolution Event**\"):

**(a)** the written consent of both Members to dissolve the Company;

**(b)** the entry of a decree of judicial dissolution under Section 18-802 of the Act;

**(c)** the occurrence of a Trigger Event as described in Section 10.01, if the buy-sell procedures set forth in Sections 10.02, 10.03, and 10.04 are initiated but not completed within the applicable time periods set forth therein (and neither Member seeks specific performance to compel completion);

**(d)** the sale, lease, exchange, or other disposition of all or substantially all of the assets of the Company (other than in the ordinary course of business);

**(e)** the occurrence of any event that makes it unlawful for the business of the Company to be carried on or for the Members to carry it on in the form of a Delaware limited liability company; or

**(f)** any other event causing the dissolution of the Company under the Act that cannot be avoided by an amendment to this Agreement or the Certificate of Formation.

**Section 14.02 --- Winding Up**

**(a)** Upon dissolution, the Management Committee (or, if the Management Committee is unable or unwilling to act, a liquidating trustee appointed by the Management Committee or, failing such appointment, by the Members) shall wind up the Company\'s affairs and liquidate its assets in an orderly manner. The Management Committee or liquidating trustee shall have full authority to take all actions necessary or appropriate to wind up the Company\'s affairs, including the power to sell Company assets, collect receivables, pay or provide for the payment of Company obligations, and distribute the remaining assets to the Members.

**(b)** The assets of the Company shall be distributed in the following order of priority:

> \(i\) **To Creditors.** First, to the payment of debts and liabilities of the Company (including debts and liabilities owed to Members who are creditors, to the extent permitted by law) and the expenses of liquidation, in the order of priority established by law.
>
> \(ii\) **To Reserves.** Second, to the establishment of such reserves as the Management Committee or liquidating trustee deems reasonably necessary for contingent, conditional, or unmatured liabilities or obligations of the Company, which reserves shall be held in an interest-bearing escrow account for such period as the Management Committee or liquidating trustee deems appropriate, after which the remaining balance of such reserves shall be distributed to the Members pursuant to clause (iii) below.
>
> \(iii\) **To Members.** Third, to the Members in accordance with their respective positive Capital Account balances, after giving effect to all Capital Contributions, allocations, and Distributions for all periods through the date of distribution.

**(c)** Notwithstanding the foregoing, upon dissolution, each Member\'s Contributed IP shall revert to the contributing Member in accordance with Sections 10.04(c) and 12.05(a), and the license-back provisions of Section 12.05 shall apply.

**Section 14.03 --- Certificate of Cancellation**

Upon the completion of the winding up of the Company\'s affairs and the distribution of all of the Company\'s assets, the Management Committee or liquidating trustee shall cause a certificate of cancellation to be filed with the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware in accordance with Section 18-203 of the Act, and shall take all other actions necessary or appropriate to terminate the existence of the Company.

**[ARTICLE XV --- BOOKS, RECORDS, AND REPORTING]{.underline}**

**Section 15.01 --- Books and Records**

**(a)** The Company shall maintain complete and accurate books of account and records of the Company\'s business and affairs at its principal office, prepared in accordance with GAAP. Such books and records shall include, without limitation, a current list of the names and addresses of all Members, copies of this Agreement and the Certificate of Formation (as amended from time to time), all federal, state, and local income tax returns and reports, all financial statements, and such other records as are required by the Act or as the Management Committee deems appropriate.

**(b)** Each Member shall have the right, during ordinary business hours and upon reasonable prior written notice to the Company (not less than five (5) business days), to inspect and copy, at such Member\'s own expense, the books and records of the Company, including the financial statements, tax returns, and any other documents reasonably related to such Member\'s interest in the Company. The Company shall make its books and records available electronically through a secure data room to the extent practicable.

**Section 15.02 --- Auditor**

**(a)** The Company\'s independent auditor shall be Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP, or such other nationally recognized independent accounting firm as the Management Committee may designate from time to time (subject to the Reserved Matters approval requirement of Section 5.02(m)).

**(b)** Annual audited financial statements of the Company, prepared in accordance with GAAP and accompanied by an unqualified opinion of the Company\'s independent auditor, shall be delivered to each Member within ninety (90) days of the end of each Fiscal Year.

**(c)** Quarterly unaudited financial statements of the Company, including a balance sheet, income statement, statement of cash flows, and such other schedules as the Management Committee may require, shall be delivered to each Member within forty-five (45) days of the end of each fiscal quarter.

**(d)** Monthly management reports, including key operating metrics, shall be delivered to the Management Committee within twenty (20) business days of the end of each calendar month.

**Section 15.03 --- Tax Returns and Reports**

**(a)** The Company shall cause to be prepared and timely filed all required federal, state, and local income tax returns and information returns of the Company, including IRS Form 1065 (U.S. Return of Partnership Income) and all associated schedules.

**(b)** The Company shall deliver IRS Schedule K-1 (or its equivalent) to each Member within ninety (90) days of the end of each Fiscal Year (or such earlier date as may be required by applicable law or reasonably requested by a Member in order to timely file its own tax returns).

**(c)** **Tax Matters Partner / Partnership Representative.** Meridian (or its designee) shall serve as the \"tax matters partner\" of the Company for purposes of the Code (to the extent applicable) and as the \"partnership representative\" under the Bipartisan Budget Act of 2015 (BBA) partnership audit rules (Subchapter C of Chapter 63 of the Code, as amended), with full authority to act on behalf of the Company in connection with any federal, state, or local income tax audit, examination, or proceeding. The partnership representative shall keep all Members informed of all material developments in any such audit, examination, or proceeding and shall not settle or otherwise resolve any audit, examination, or proceeding without the prior written consent of Kastor (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed). If the Company is subject to an \"imputed underpayment\" under the BBA partnership audit rules, the partnership representative shall use commercially reasonable efforts to elect the \"push-out\" election under Section 6226 of the Code, so that adjustments are pushed out to the Members rather than being paid at the Company level.

**[ARTICLE XVI --- CONFIDENTIALITY]{.underline}**

**Section 16.01 --- Confidential Information**

\"**Confidential Information**\" means all non-public information (whether written, oral, electronic, or visual) relating to the Company, its business, operations, products, technology, financial condition, plans, strategies, customers, suppliers, employees, or the terms of this Agreement, including all Intellectual Property, trade secrets, know-how, formulations, specifications, financial statements, projections, business plans, marketing strategies, customer lists, supplier agreements, pricing information, and any other information that is proprietary or competitively sensitive. Confidential Information shall not include information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the receiving Member or its representatives in violation of this Article XVI, (b) was available to the receiving Member on a non-confidential basis prior to its disclosure by the disclosing Member or the Company, (c) becomes available to the receiving Member on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the disclosing Member or the Company, *provided* that such source is not bound by a confidentiality agreement or other obligation of secrecy with respect to such information, or (d) is independently developed by the receiving Member without use of or reference to Confidential Information.

**Section 16.02 --- Obligations**

Each Member shall (a) maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information of the Company and the other Member and shall not disclose or permit the disclosure of such Confidential Information to any third party, and (b) use Confidential Information solely for purposes of performing its obligations and exercising its rights under this Agreement and in connection with the business and affairs of the Company. Notwithstanding the foregoing, a Member may disclose Confidential Information:

> \(i\) to its Affiliates, officers, directors, employees, agents, advisors (including attorneys, accountants, consultants, and financial advisors), and lenders, in each case on a need-to-know basis and subject to such recipient\'s agreement to maintain the confidentiality of such information on terms no less restrictive than those set forth in this Article XVI;
>
> \(ii\) as required by applicable law, rule, regulation, or legal process (including by subpoena, civil investigative demand, or similar process), *provided* that the disclosing Member shall, to the extent permitted by law, provide the other Member with prompt written notice of any such requirement so that the other Member may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, and *provided further* that the disclosing Member shall disclose only that portion of the Confidential Information that is legally required to be disclosed and shall use commercially reasonable efforts to obtain confidential treatment for any Confidential Information so disclosed; or
>
> \(iii\) with the prior written consent of the other Member.

**Section 16.03 --- Duration**

The confidentiality obligations set forth in this Article XVI shall survive the dissolution of the Company, the termination of this Agreement, and the exit of any Member, for a period of five (5) years following the later of (a) the dissolution of the Company and (b) the date on which a Member ceases to hold any interest in the Company. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the confidentiality obligations with respect to trade secrets shall continue for so long as such information constitutes a trade secret under applicable law.

**Section 16.04 --- Equitable Relief**

Each Member acknowledges and agrees that any breach of the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Article XVI would cause irreparable harm to the Company and the other Member, that monetary damages alone would not be an adequate remedy for such breach, and that the non-breaching Member and the Company shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief (including temporary restraining orders, preliminary injunctions, and permanent injunctions) to enforce the provisions of this Article XVI, without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bond or other security. The right to seek equitable relief shall be in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other rights or remedies available at law or in equity.

**[ARTICLE XVII --- REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES, AND COVENANTS]{.underline}**

**Section 17.01 --- Mutual Representations**

Each Member hereby represents and warrants to the other Member, as of the Effective Date, as follows:

**(a)** Such Member is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing (or the equivalent thereof under applicable law) under the laws of its jurisdiction of organization.

**(b)** Such Member has full power and authority to execute, deliver, and perform this Agreement. The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate, limited liability company, or other organizational action of such Member.

**(c)** This Agreement constitutes the legal, valid, and binding obligation of such Member, enforceable against such Member in accordance with its terms, except as enforcement may be limited by applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium, or similar laws affecting creditors\' rights generally, or by general equitable principles (regardless of whether enforcement is sought in a proceeding at law or in equity).

**(d)** The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by such Member do not and will not conflict with, violate, or result in a breach of (i) the organizational documents of such Member, (ii) any material agreement, contract, or instrument to which such Member is a party or by which it is bound, or (iii) any law, rule, regulation, order, judgment, or decree applicable to such Member.

**(e)** All consents, approvals, authorizations, and filings required in connection with the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement by such Member have been obtained or made and are in full force and effect.

**(f)** There is no action, suit, proceeding, investigation, or claim pending or, to such Member\'s knowledge, threatened against such Member that would, individually or in the aggregate, reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on such Member\'s ability to perform its obligations under this Agreement.

**Section 17.02 --- Meridian Representations**

Meridian hereby represents and warrants to Kastor, as of the Effective Date, as follows:

**(a)** Meridian is the sole legal and beneficial owner of the Meridian Contributed IP, free and clear of all liens, claims, encumbrances, security interests, and restrictions, and has full right, title, power, and authority to contribute, assign, and transfer the Meridian Contributed IP to the Company.

**(b)** The independent appraisal of the Meridian Contributed IP conducted by Frazier Whitmore & Co., LLP was prepared in good faith and in accordance with generally accepted valuation methodologies, and the agreed fair market value of Twenty-Eight Million Six Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$28,600,000) reflects a reasonable estimate of the fair market value of the Meridian Contributed IP as of the date of such appraisal.

**(c)** There are no undisclosed encumbrances, licenses, options, or other rights granted to any third party with respect to the Meridian Contributed IP that would conflict with or restrict the Company\'s use of such IP in furtherance of the Business Purpose.

**(d)** Meridian has disclosed to Kastor all material information in its possession relating to the Meridian Contributed IP, including any known prior art, third-party claims, or potential infringement issues.

**Section 17.03 --- Kastor Representations**

Kastor hereby represents and warrants to Meridian, as of the Effective Date, as follows:

**(a)** Kastor is duly organized and validly existing under the laws of the Canton of Zug, Switzerland, and has full corporate power and authority under Swiss law (including the Swiss Code of Obligations) to execute, deliver, and perform this Agreement and to make the Capital Contributions required hereunder.

**(b)** Kastor is in compliance with all applicable Swiss foreign direct investment regulations, export control regulations (including the Swiss War Material Act (*Kriegsmaterialgesetz*) and the Swiss Goods Control Act (*Güterkontrollgesetz*)), and sanctions regulations applicable to Kastor\'s contribution of IP and capital to the Company and to its participation in the JV.

**(c)** Kastor is the sole legal and beneficial owner of the Kastor Contributed IP, free and clear of all liens, claims, encumbrances, security interests, and restrictions, and has full right, title, power, and authority to contribute, assign, and transfer the Kastor Contributed IP to the Company.

**(d)** The terms of the Supply Agreement to be entered into between Kastor and the Company pursuant to Section 13.01 have been negotiated in good faith and on arm\'s-length terms, and the agreed value of Two Million Dollars (\$2,000,000) attributed to the initial term of the Supply Agreement represents a reasonable estimate of the value of the commitments and preferential terms embodied therein.

**(e)** Kastor has disclosed to Meridian all material information in its possession relating to the Kastor Contributed IP, including any known prior art, third-party claims, or potential infringement issues.

**Section 17.04 --- Ongoing Covenants**

Each Member hereby covenants and agrees, for so long as it holds any interest in the Company, as follows:

**(a) Cooperation.** Each Member shall cooperate fully with the other Member and with the Company in connection with all matters relating to the Company\'s business, including the prosecution and maintenance of the Company\'s IP portfolio, the resolution of disputes, and the preparation and filing of tax returns and regulatory filings.

**(b) Further Assurances.** Each Member shall execute and deliver such additional documents, instruments, conveyances, and assurances, and take such further actions, as may be reasonably required to carry out the provisions of this Agreement and give effect to the transactions contemplated hereby.

**(c) Regulatory Compliance.** Each Member shall maintain compliance in all material respects with all applicable laws, rules, and regulations relevant to the Company\'s business and such Member\'s participation in the JV, including, without limitation, applicable United States federal, state, and local laws and regulations (including export control and sanctions regulations administered by OFAC, BIS, and other governmental authorities) and, with respect to Kastor, applicable Swiss and European Union laws and regulations.

**(d) Insurance.** The Company shall obtain and maintain, at the Company\'s expense, customary insurance coverage for the Company\'s business and operations, including general liability insurance, product liability insurance, property insurance, directors and officers (D&O) liability insurance covering Managers, and such other coverage as the Management Committee deems appropriate. Each Member shall cooperate with the Company in obtaining and maintaining such insurance, including by providing information reasonably requested by the Company\'s insurers.

**(e) Notification of Material Events.** Each Member shall promptly notify the other Member and the Company of any event, circumstance, or development that could reasonably be expected to have a material adverse effect on (i) the Company\'s business, financial condition, or operations, (ii) the value of the Company\'s assets (including its IP portfolio), or (iii) such Member\'s ability to perform its obligations under this Agreement.

**[ARTICLE XVIII --- INDEMNIFICATION]{.underline}**

**Section 18.01 --- Indemnification by Meridian**

Meridian shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Company, Kastor, and their respective Affiliates, and each of their respective officers, directors, managers, employees, agents, successors, and assigns (collectively, the \"**Kastor Indemnified Parties**\"), from and against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees and expenses, costs of investigation, and amounts paid in settlement) (collectively, \"**Losses**\") arising out of, resulting from, or relating to:

**(a)** any breach of any representation, warranty, or covenant of Meridian set forth in this Agreement;

**(b)** any negligence, gross negligence, or willful misconduct of Meridian or any of its Affiliates, officers, directors, employees, agents, or representatives in connection with the Company\'s business or this Agreement; or

**(c)** any third-party claim arising from Meridian\'s Contributed IP, including any claim alleging that the Meridian Contributed IP infringes, misappropriates, or otherwise violates the Intellectual Property rights of any third party, to the extent such claim relates to the Meridian Contributed IP as it existed on the date of contribution to the Company (and not to any Improvements or Co-Developed IP created by the Company after such contribution).

**Section 18.02 --- Indemnification by Kastor**

Kastor shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Company, Meridian, and their respective Affiliates, and each of their respective officers, directors, managers, employees, agents, successors, and assigns (collectively, the \"**Meridian Indemnified Parties**\"), from and against any and all Losses arising out of, resulting from, or relating to:

**(a)** any breach of any representation, warranty, or covenant of Kastor set forth in this Agreement;

**(b)** any negligence, gross negligence, or willful misconduct of Kastor or any of its Affiliates, officers, directors, employees, agents, or representatives in connection with the Company\'s business or this Agreement; or

**(c)** any third-party claim arising from Kastor\'s Contributed IP, including any claim alleging that the Kastor Contributed IP infringes, misappropriates, or otherwise violates the Intellectual Property rights of any third party, to the extent such claim relates to the Kastor Contributed IP as it existed on the date of contribution to the Company (and not to any Improvements or Co-Developed IP created by the Company after such contribution).

**Section 18.03 --- Indemnification Procedures**

**(a) Notice.** The party seeking indemnification (the \"**Indemnified Party**\") shall promptly notify the indemnifying party (the \"**Indemnifying Party**\") in writing of any claim, action, suit, or proceeding for which indemnification is sought (a \"**Claim**\"), setting forth in reasonable detail the facts and circumstances giving rise to such Claim and the amount or estimated amount of Losses (to the extent known). The failure of the Indemnified Party to provide timely notice shall not relieve the Indemnifying Party of its indemnification obligations except to the extent that the Indemnifying Party is materially prejudiced by such failure.

**(b) Defense of Claims.** The Indemnifying Party shall have the right, at its sole cost and expense, to assume the defense of any Claim, using counsel reasonably acceptable to the Indemnified Party, by delivering written notice to the Indemnified Party within thirty (30) days after receipt of the notice of the Claim. If the Indemnifying Party assumes the defense of a Claim, the Indemnified Party shall have the right to participate in such defense at its own expense and with its own counsel, *provided* that the Indemnifying Party shall retain control of the defense and settlement of such Claim. If the Indemnifying Party does not assume the defense of a Claim within such thirty (30)-day period, the Indemnified Party may assume the defense at the Indemnifying Party\'s cost and expense.

**(c) Cooperation.** Each party shall cooperate fully with the other party in connection with the defense, settlement, or resolution of any Claim, including by providing access to relevant documents, records, and personnel.

**(d) Settlement.** The Indemnifying Party shall not settle or compromise any Claim without the prior written consent of the Indemnified Party (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed), unless such settlement (i) involves only the payment of money (and does not require the Indemnified Party to admit fault or take or refrain from taking any action), and (ii) includes a complete and unconditional release of the Indemnified Party from all liability relating to such Claim.

**(e) Limitations on Liability.**

> \(i\) **Cap.** The aggregate liability of each Indemnifying Party under this Article XVIII shall not exceed such Indemnifying Party\'s Capital Contribution to the Company, *provided* that this cap shall not apply to Losses arising from fraud, willful misconduct, or a knowing violation of law.
>
> \(ii\) **Basket.** No Indemnifying Party shall be liable for any Losses under this Article XVIII unless the aggregate amount of all Losses for which such Indemnifying Party would otherwise be liable exceeds Five Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$500,000) (the \"**Basket**\"), in which case the Indemnifying Party shall be liable for all Losses in excess of the Basket (subject to the cap set forth in clause (i) above). The Basket shall not apply to Losses arising from fraud, willful misconduct, or a knowing violation of law.
>
> \(iii\) **Time Limitation.** No claim for indemnification under this Article XVIII (other than a claim arising from fraud, willful misconduct, or a knowing violation of law) may be asserted after the date that is twenty-four (24) months following the Effective Date (with respect to claims arising from breaches of representations and warranties) or twenty-four (24) months following the date of the alleged breach (with respect to claims arising from breaches of covenants).

**(f) Exclusive Remedy.** The indemnification provisions of this Article XVIII shall constitute the sole and exclusive remedy of the Members with respect to any Losses arising out of or relating to breaches of representations, warranties, and covenants set forth in this Agreement, except for claims for specific performance, injunctive relief, or other equitable remedies as expressly provided elsewhere in this Agreement, and except for claims arising from fraud, willful misconduct, or a knowing violation of law.

**[ARTICLE XIX --- DISPUTE RESOLUTION]{.underline}**

**Section 19.01 --- Negotiation**

The Members shall first attempt to resolve any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of, relating to, or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination, or validity thereof (a \"**Dispute**\"), through good-faith negotiation between their designated representatives (initially, Elaine Cordero for Meridian and Dr. Henrik Laufer for Kastor, or such other representatives as each Member may designate from time to time). Either Member may initiate the negotiation process by delivering written notice to the other Member describing the Dispute in reasonable detail (the \"**Dispute Notice**\"). The designated representatives shall meet (in person or by videoconference) within fifteen (15) days after delivery of the Dispute Notice and shall use commercially reasonable efforts to resolve the Dispute.

**Section 19.02 --- Mediation**

If the Dispute is not resolved through negotiation within thirty (30) days after delivery of the Dispute Notice (or such longer period as the Members may agree in writing), either Member may submit the Dispute to mediation administered by JAMS in New York, New York, in accordance with JAMS\'s then-current mediation rules and procedures. The mediation shall be conducted by a single mediator mutually selected by the Members (or, if the Members cannot agree on a mediator within ten (10) days after submission to JAMS, appointed by JAMS in accordance with its rules). The mediation shall be conducted in English and shall be completed within sixty (60) days following the appointment of the mediator, unless the Members agree in writing to extend such period. The costs of the mediation (including the mediator\'s fees and expenses) shall be shared equally by the Members, with each Member bearing its own attorneys\' fees and expenses.

**Section 19.03 --- Arbitration or Litigation**

**(a)** If the Dispute is not resolved through mediation within sixty (60) days after the appointment of the mediator (or such longer period as the Members may agree in writing), either Member may initiate binding arbitration or proceed to litigation, as set forth below.

**(b) Arbitration.** If the Members have agreed (whether in this Agreement, as finally executed, or by separate written agreement at the time of the Dispute) that Disputes shall be resolved by arbitration, such arbitration shall be conducted under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce (the \"**ICC Rules**\") then in effect, subject to the following:

> \(i\) The arbitral tribunal shall consist of three (3) arbitrators. Each Member shall nominate one (1) arbitrator, and the two party-nominated arbitrators shall jointly select the third arbitrator, who shall serve as the presiding arbitrator. If the two party-nominated arbitrators cannot agree on the presiding arbitrator within twenty (20) days after their appointment, the presiding arbitrator shall be appointed by the ICC International Court of Arbitration in accordance with the ICC Rules.
>
> \(ii\) The seat (legal place) of arbitration shall be New York, New York.
>
> \(iii\) The language of the arbitration shall be English.
>
> \(iv\) The arbitral tribunal shall render a final award within twelve (12) months after the constitution of the tribunal, unless the tribunal determines that the complexity of the Dispute requires an extension of such period.
>
> \(v\) The award of the arbitral tribunal shall be final and binding on the Members and may be enforced in any court of competent jurisdiction, including pursuant to the Convention on the Recognition and Enforcement of Foreign Arbitral Awards (New York Convention, 1958).
>
> \(vi\) The arbitral tribunal shall have the authority to award compensatory damages, specific performance, injunctive relief, and such other remedies as may be available under applicable law, *provided* that the arbitral tribunal shall not have the authority to award punitive, consequential, or exemplary damages.

**(c) Litigation.** If the Members have not agreed to arbitration (or if the final version of this Agreement does not include an arbitration provision), either Member may bring an action in the Court of Chancery of the State of Delaware (or, if the Court of Chancery declines jurisdiction, the Superior Court of the State of Delaware), and each Member hereby irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction and venue of such courts for purposes of any such action. Each Member waives any objection it may have to the laying of venue in such courts and agrees not to assert the defense of forum non conveniens.

**(d)** The selection of arbitration versus litigation as the final Dispute resolution mechanism for this Agreement is subject to further agreement of the Members and their respective counsel prior to the execution of the final version of this Agreement.

**Section 19.04 --- Provisional Remedies**

Nothing in this Article XIX shall preclude or limit the right of either Member to seek provisional, interim, or conservatory measures (including temporary restraining orders, preliminary injunctions, attachments, or other forms of interim relief) from any court of competent jurisdiction, at any time before, during, or after the pendency of any negotiation, mediation, arbitration, or litigation proceeding. Any such application for provisional measures shall not be deemed a waiver of the right to arbitrate or litigate the underlying Dispute, as applicable.

**Section 20.10 --- Expenses**

Each Member shall bear its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with the negotiation, preparation, execution, and delivery of this Agreement and the consummation of the transactions contemplated hereby, including all fees and expenses of its own counsel, accountants, financial advisors, and other representatives. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the costs and expenses of forming the Company (including filing fees for the Certificate of Formation and registered agent fees) shall be borne by the Company.

**Section 20.11 --- No Third-Party Beneficiaries**

As set forth in Section 3.03, this Agreement is for the sole benefit of the Members and their respective successors and permitted assigns and is not intended to confer any rights, benefits, or remedies upon any other Person. Nothing in this Agreement, express or implied, shall be construed to create any third-party beneficiary rights in any Person other than the Members and their respective successors and permitted assigns.

**Section 20.12 --- Waiver of Jury Trial**

**EACH MEMBER HEREBY IRREVOCABLY AND UNCONDITIONALLY WAIVES, TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ANY RIGHT IT MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY ACTION, SUIT, PROCEEDING, OR COUNTERCLAIM (WHETHER BASED ON CONTRACT, TORT, OR OTHERWISE) ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, THE TRANSACTIONS CONTEMPLATED HEREBY, OR THE ACTIONS OF ANY MEMBER IN THE NEGOTIATION, ADMINISTRATION, PERFORMANCE, OR ENFORCEMENT OF THIS AGREEMENT.** Each Member acknowledges and agrees that it has been induced to enter into this Agreement by, among other things, the mutual waivers and certifications set forth in this Section 20.12.

*\[Remainder of this page intentionally left blank. Signature pages follow.\]*
